key_insight: Thermal spray coatings can be engineered at the micro- to nanoscale to tune porosity, phase distribution, and interfacial chemistry, improving electrochemical performance and durability across batteries and fuel/electrolysis systems.
engineering_relevance: A unified process-structure-property framework helps translate thermal spray parameters into more reliable, scalable, and defect-tolerant functional interfaces for energy devices.
method: Compared atmospheric plasma spray, suspension plasma spray, high-velocity oxy-fuel, and cold spray.
Linked process settings to coating microstructure, porosity, phase evolution, and interfacial resistance.
Synthesized cross-system design principles for batteries, PEM, alkaline fuel cells, and SOFC/SOECs.
evidence_signal: The paper is a review that synthesizes prior studies rather than reporting new experimental datasets, using cross-system comparisons to support the proposed framework.
watch_item: Performance gains may be limited by transport penalties from excessive porosity, weak inter-splat bonding, and non-equilibrium phase changes during spraying.
paper_title: Thermal spray coatings in batteries, electrolyzers and solid oxide electrochemical systems
